mission.title: containerd: 'failed to reserve container name'
mission.description: 'failed to reserve container name'. This issue affects 28+ users.

steps.title: Identify containerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your containerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
**Description**

Hi! 

We are running containerd on GKE with pretty much all defaults. A dozen nodes, and a few hundreds pods. Plenty of memory and disk free.

steps.title: Apply the fix for 'failed to reserve container name'
steps.description:
jotting down some notes here, apologies if it's lengthy:  

Let me try to explain/figure out the reason you got "failed to reserve container name" .. 

Kubelet tried to create a container that it had already asked containerd to create at least once.. when containerd tried the first time it received
```yaml
2020-10-07T08:01:45Z Successfully assigned default/apps-abcd-6b6cb5876b-nn9md to gke-bap-mtl-1-preemptible-e2-s4-e6a8ddb4-ng3v I 
2020-10-07T08:01:50Z Pulling image "gcr.io/my/appImage:223c133ff631c41e1bc21a8b7d7554036da4fb4e" I 
2020-10-07T08:16:45Z Successfully pulled image "gcr.io/my/appImage:223c133ff631c41e1bc21a8b7d7554036da4fb4e" I 
2020-10-07T08:18:45Z Error: context deadline exceeded W 
2020-10-07T08:18:45Z Container image "redis:4.0-alpine" already present on machine I 
2020-10-07T08:18:53Z Created container redis I 
2020-10-07T08:18:53Z Started container redis I 
2020-10-07T08:18:53
```

steps.title: Confirm 'failed to reserve container name' is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
jotting down some notes here, apologies if it's lengthy:  

Let me try to explain/figure out the reason you got "failed to reserve container name" .. 

Kubelet tried to create a container that it had already asked containerd to create at least once..

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
